title: Sour Chewy Canadian Pork Chops
description: A high-protein-friendly brunch dish featuring tender pork chops with a tangy glaze that's perfect for those seeking a savory yet refreshing meal.

ingredients.name: Sour Cream
ingredients.notes: Whole milk sour cream for a tangy flavor

instructions: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Season the pork chops with salt and pepper on both sides.
In a small bowl, mix together cornstarch, Dijon mustard, apple cider vinegar, and honey. Slowly pour in olive oil while stirring until fully combined into a smooth glaze.
Heat some olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Once hot, add the pork chops skin-side down for about 4 minutes or until they develop a nice sear. Flip and brush with the prepared glaze mixture.
Transfer the skillet to your preheated oven and continue baking for approximately 20-30 minutes, flipping halfway through cooking time to ensure even browning on all sides.
Once done, remove from oven and let rest for a few minutes before slicing into individual pieces. Serve hot with steamed vegetables or side salad.
